# Abstract {.unnumbered}

The Computer Organization (EC) course in the Computer Engineering degree at FIB-UPC had used MIPS as its reference architecture since its inception. The declining industrial relevance of this ISA and the consolidation of RISC-V as an open, de facto standard — adopted by the European Union and by the authors of the leading textbooks in the field — motivated the comprehensive migration of the course's written teaching materials.

The goal was to revise and update the entire written corpus (lecture notes, problem sets, solution manuals and lab materials) replacing MIPS with RISC-V, while simultaneously transforming the material format from closed PDF to Quarto Markdown hosted in a UPC GitLab repository. The methodology is structured in two sequential stages: the **Production Stage**, encompassing system design, corpus generation and its revision — first individually and then collegially with the full teaching team —, and the **Exploitation Stage**, iterative by semester, which incorporates classroom feedback and produces teaching support materials. Within the Production Stage, AI-assisted production using Claude was the decisive acceleration factor, multiplying speed and coverage beyond initial projections.

The results exceed initial expectations: 100% of the course corpus has been completed — lecture notes, problem sets, solution manuals and lab materials — and the External Review is under way, with protocol, schedule and working groups established. A distinctive feature of the project is the systematic adoption of the *Definition of Done* (DoD) concept, adapted from the Scrum framework, to ensure quality at each phase: verifiable binary criteria for the production of each teaching unit (DoD-UP), for the individual full-corpus review (DoD-CC) and for the collegial review (DoD-UR). This orthogonality in the definition of work units — with deliberately different granularities at each phase — enables thorough scrutiny and criterion standardisation across the three project axes: ISA transition (OE1), format transformation (OE2) and quality system establishment (OE3), making the process replicable and auditable.

The project demonstrates that combining an open, structured format, expert supervision of generative AI and an agile methodology multiplies the capacity for quality teaching material production — 36 files and over 100 SVG figures within the planned schedule — and offers an exportable model for other STEAM subjects.

**Keywords**: RISC-V, computer organization, computer architecture, Quarto Markdown, agile methodology, AI-assisted learning.

**Website**: [https://loi.pc.ac.upc.edu/ec](https://loi.pc.ac.upc.edu/ec)

**Repository**: [https://gitlab.upc.edu/ec](https://gitlab.upc.edu/ec) (requires [UPClink](https://serveistic.upc.edu/ca/upclink) VPN)

\newpage
